objective: Deliver a deterministic async service registry and supervisor with dependency ordering, bounded start/stop, rollback on partial startup failure, reverse graceful shutdown and an owner-visible demo supervising the real durable store and local control plane.
non_goals: Do not implement Windows services, containers, distributed orchestration, automatic process spawning or public network deployment.
Do not implement model inference, speech, memory, avatar, game or livestream services.
Do not run the 100-cycle deep lifecycle gate during normal fast development; provide it as an explicit owner command.
contracts: Service names are unique, dependencies exist and the registry must be an acyclic graph before startup.
Startup follows topological dependency order and a dependent never starts before every dependency reports healthy.
Every start and stop operation has a monotonic timeout; partial startup failure rolls back all services that already started in reverse order.
Graceful shutdown is reverse topological, continues after individual stop failures and leaves no service marked running.
Start and stop are idempotent at the supervisor boundary and registry mutation is forbidden while the supervisor is active.
Caught lifecycle failures use stable error codes and write redacted local JSONL records without replacing the original exception.
allowed_dependencies: Python 3.12+ standard library and existing local hina-core runtime modules.
acceptance_criteria: Unit tests prove dependency order, reverse shutdown, duplicate/missing/cycle rejection, startup rollback and stop-failure continuation.
A fast multi-cycle unit test returns every service to stopped state without retained asyncio tasks.
A single demo command supervises the real SQLite store and loopback control server, reads /v1/health and shuts both down cleanly.
A separate explicit command can run 100 lifecycle cycles and returns nonzero on any leaked running state or ordering failure.
Focused M01 fast tests complete once on the owner machine.
